text: Developing a mobile app involves several key decisions. First, responsive design ensures your app looks good and works well on different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. You'll also need to choose between native development, building separate apps for iOS and Android, and cross-platform tools like React Native that let you write one codebase for both systems. This affects performance: native apps often run faster and smoother. Finally, once your app is ready, you must submit it to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store, which have specific guidelines. A successful launch requires careful planning to balance cost, performance, and user experience.
